summary: Multi-Tenant Hospital Management System (HMS) web app using React.js, Node.js + Express, MongoDB, and JWT. Supports multiple hospitals with isolated data. Four roles: Super Admin, Hospital Admin, Doctor, Patient. Includes medicine management, real-time stock tracking, expiry monitoring, low stock alerts, supplier management, bed management, blood bank, doctor management, appointment system, reports dashboard, and role-based access control. Separate dashboards per role. Bonus: dark mode, search/filter, PDF export, email notifications.
architectural_notes: Stack: React.js frontend, Node.js + Express backend, MongoDB database, JWT authentication
Multi-tenancy via hospitalId field scoping all tenant-specific collections (Users, Medicines, Suppliers, Beds, BloodStock, Appointments, Reports)
MongoDB collections: Users, Hospitals, Medicines, Suppliers, Beds, BloodStock, Appointments, Reports
Role hierarchy: superadmin > hospitalAdmin > doctor > patient; middleware enforces role + tenant checks on every API route
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- Minimalist teal and white hospital cross logo icon on a clean rounded square background (hms-logo-icon.dim_128x128.png)
- Modern medical dashboard hero illustration showing abstract hospital building silhouette with teal gradient and subtle data visualization elements (hms-hero-illustration.dim_1200x600.png)

existing_features.title: Implement Internet Identity authentication as the sole login method, replacing JWT. Map roles (Super Admin, Hospital Admin, Doctor, Patient) to principals stored in the backend. Enforce role-based access control so each principal can only access endpoints and data permitted by their role.

Build a Super Admin dashboard page showing: total number of hospitals, list of hospitals with status, and system-wide report summaries. Super Admin can create new hospitals and assign Hospital Admin principals to them.

Build a Hospital Admin dashboard page with sidebar navigation linking to: Medicines, Suppliers, Beds, Blood Bank, Doctors, Appointments, Staff, and Reports sections. Dashboard home shows summary stat cards for each module.

Build a Doctor dashboard page showing: list of assigned patients with appointment details, a form to update patient treatment notes and deduct medicine usage, and a read-only medicine availability view.

Build a Patient dashboard page allowing patients to: book appointments by browsing doctors and available slots, view their own appointments, check bed availability, check blood group availability, and view their prescribed medicines.

Implement in-app alert notifications: display a notification badge/panel showing low stock medicine alerts, expiring medicines (within 7 days), low blood stock alerts, and any other system warnings. Alerts are scoped to the logged-in user's hospital.
Implement dark mode toggle: users can switch between light and dark themes. The preference is persisted in localStorage and applied on page load.
Implement search and filter controls on all data tables (Medicines, Suppliers, Beds, Blood Bank, Doctors, Appointments). Search is client-side for the currently loaded page data.
Implement client-side PDF export for reports: on each report page, a button triggers a PDF download of the currently displayed report data using a frontend PDF library.

Apply a clean, modern medical-grade visual theme: use a white and teal/green primary palette with crisp sans-serif typography, card-based layouts with subtle shadows, a collapsible sidebar, and consistent status-color coding (red for alerts, green for available, amber for warnings). Enforce this theme consistently across all pages and both light/dark modes.
Generate a hospital dashboard logo/icon and a hero illustration for the login/landing page.

new_feature_requests.summary: Multi-tenancy: isolated data per hospital
Role-based access control: Super Admin, Hospital Admin, Doctor, Patient
JWT authentication with password encryption
Medicine management: CRUD, stock tracking, low stock alerts, expiry monitoring
Supplier management: add/edit suppliers, track supplier medicines
Bed management: total/available/occupied/ICU beds tracking
Blood bank management: blood group availability, quantity, low stock alerts
Doctor management: add doctors, availability, specialization, appointment scheduling
Appointment system: patient booking, doctor availability calendar
Reports dashboard: medicine usage, stock, expired medicines, bed occupancy, blood stock; PDF export
Separate dashboards for Super Admin, Hospital Admin, Doctor, Patient
Bonus: dark mode, search/filter, email notifications

Implement multi-tenancy in the backend: every Hospital Admin, Doctor, Supplier, Medicine, Bed, BloodStock, and Appointment record must carry a hospitalId. All queries must be scoped to the caller's hospitalId so data from one hospital is never visible to another hospital.

Create backend data models and CRUD operations for: Users, Hospitals, Medicines (with stock quantity, minimum quantity, expiry date, supplierId), Suppliers, Beds (type: general/ICU, status: available/occupied), BloodStock (blood group, quantity, minimum quantity), Appointments (patientId, doctorId, hospitalId, datetime, status), and Reports (aggregated snapshots).

Implement Medicine Management: Hospital Admins can add, update, and delete medicines. Track current stock quantity. Expose a query that returns medicines with stock below minimum quantity (low stock alert). Expose a query that returns medicines whose expiry date has passed or is within 7 days (expiry alert).
Implement Supplier Management: Hospital Admins can add and edit suppliers. Each medicine record can reference a supplierId. Expose a query returning all medicines linked to a given supplier.

Implement Bed Management: Hospital Admins can add beds with type (General or ICU) and toggle status (Available/Occupied). Expose queries for total beds, available beds, occupied beds, and ICU bed counts per hospital.

Implement Blood Bank Management: Hospital Admins can manage blood group inventory (A+, A-, B+, B-, AB+, AB-, O+, O-) with quantity and minimum threshold. Expose a query returning blood groups below minimum quantity.
Implement Doctor Management: Hospital Admins can add doctors with specialization and set their availability schedule. Expose queries for doctor list and availability per hospital.

Implement the Appointment System: Patients can book appointments by selecting a doctor and available time slot. Doctors can view their upcoming appointments. Hospital Admins can view all appointments for their hospital.

Implement a Reports module: backend exposes aggregated report queries for medicine usage, current stock, expired medicines, bed occupancy rate, and blood stock levels — all scoped per hospital. Super Admin can request reports across all hospitals.
